items.title: 1 Button Deadeye Power 29k Realistic
items.rotation: Set your pistol 3 skill to auto cast. Just use your mark that's it.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity thief build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic == 10 boons on you, 10 conditions on golem. You have traits that scale with number of boons on you and the number of conditions on golem.

I decided to post this one even though others have this build. I saw that others were running other utilities or sometimes the initiative regeneration signet. However those versions yielded me ~28k DPS. I switched to impact instead and ran full zerkers with precision signet to get 29k. So a very slight increase if you plan on running this build as a 1 buttoner.

items.title: 3 Button Antiquary Power 39.9k Realistic
items.rotation: Set Pistol 3 to auto cast (ctrl right click).
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity thief build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic == 10 conditions on golem. You have traits that scale with number  of conditions on golem.

0:00 Rotation
2:02 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. Just to preface, I did not create this build. I actually had a guildie tell me this build existed and link me the build, and then I was looking online and found some stuff from Syrma. So I don't know who created this build, but shout out to whoever did. If you know who created it, please post them in the comments.

For the rundown, more or less what you want to do is spam F2. You get two artifacts. One of them is always the offensive one, and one of them is always the defensive one. So you more or less always want to be spamming F2. Your opener is F1 and 3 at the same time. That way, whenever you're not pressing F2, your Unload goes. Otherwise, if you don't, you end up in this awkward situation where after your opener, where you use your F1, F2, your elite, and then your second F2, you're basically just standing there because the Unload doesn't start until you actually press it. So I would recommend pressing F1 and 3 at kind of the same time during your opener, so any gaps in your opener are filled in by 3. More or less all you want to do is spam F2 off cooldown, use your elite whenever it's up, and then use your F1 and F2 whenever they're up. Pretty much artifacts always take precedence over Pistol 3, so you can interrupt it at any time to be spamming your F2.

The only thing about F2 is that you want to be careful with the Forged Surfer Dash artifact. If you point it in the wrong direction, your character won't face the right way and the automatic Unload will stop. All you want to make sure you're doing is having your mouse cursor pointed a little bit in front of the enemy that you're shooting. That way, whenever you dash, you're still facing the enemy and Pistol 3 keeps going.

Apart from that, Pistol 4 is a CC. You're kind of locked in on your utilities. There's some good utility out of your defensive artifacts, but that's not something you can rely on. You probably never want to switch off Signet of Malice. Some of the stuff in this build will actually hit you and do damage to you, so having some extra healing there is probably helpful. You also probably just want to leave Assassin's Signet and Signet of Agility as they are. Skritt Scuffle is not going away. This is major in this build. Do not swap this to Basilisk Venom or you won't do nearly as much damage. If you want to swap anything, you can swap Infiltrator's Signet. There's some good stuff in here. Shadowstep is really nice on fights where you have to be at range, pick something up, and then go back. Smoke Screen is technically a projectile block. There's also Shadow Portal. For the most part, thief utility is very selfish and damage-oriented, so there isn't a whole bunch of really useful utility, but you do have a lot of mobility. If you're going to replace anything, you should replace Infiltrator's Signet.

One more thing I wanted to mention: the damage on this build is a bit RNG. I'm going to post a pretty good run to showcase the maximum potential of it, but you're most likely going to get a lower number on average. If you're wondering why you're lower, it's because the build is a bit RNG with the artifacts that you get. That's all I got. I hope you enjoy the build, and I hope you all don't blow yourselves up out there. Thank you.

items.title: 3 Button Deadeye Power 33.2k Realistic
items.rotation: Mark, spam staff 2, make sure to trigger Maleficent Seven, use Mercy to reset malice and initiative. Make sure to not drop your mark so use it throughout.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity thief build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic == 10 boons on you, 10 conditions on golem. You have traits that scale with number of boons on you and the number of conditions on golem.

items.title: 3 Button Deadeye Condi 32.7k Realistic
items.rotation: Deadeye's mark into Dagger 3. Mercy once you trigger Maleficent Seven and run out of initiative to regain initiative and reset Maleficent Seven. Recast deadeye's mark but make sure you get back the initiative from it.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity thief build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic == 10 boons on you, 10 conditions on golem. You have traits that scale with number of boons on you and the number of conditions on golem.

It wasn't the cleanest run but it was the highest number I got. Also as a warning this one seems kind of dangerous to play since there is a lot of movement locks. Play at your own risk.
